mechanics.text:
SKILLS
well numerous narrower versions of these subskills_ For example , characters rarely study such broad topic 'History;' but would focus their knowledge on subskills such as Star League History" or "History of New Avalon_ Sample Academic Subskills: Philosophy; Psychology; Theology; History; Anthropology; Archaeology; Law; Literature Agriculture_ Biology; Astronomy, Geography, Linguistics_ Mythology: Political Science, Sociology Statistics
For the most effective training; appropriate species of animal must be started on training at very young age At the end of every month in which the animal is trained for at least an hour day; make an Animal Handling Check _ with difficulty mod- ifiers based on the kind of aimal being trained (cats are noto riously hard to train; while dogs are well-known for their obedi- ence). Success means the animal is taking the  training: Failure means the animal is not, and another Check must be made in the following month; After successful Animal Handling Check, second Check can be made t0 see the animal learns new command with the difficulty based on the complexity of the command: Success means the animal has learned the command; failure means nothing new was learned An animal can Iearn limited number of tricks depending on its intelligence_ at the gamemaster' dis cretion.
ACROBATICS This skill allows character t0 move his body quickly and gracefully through complicated maneuvers_ Acrobatics gives character better chance of accomplishing any action requiring physical agility; such as jumping from moving vehicle, leaping between rooftops and the like. Acrobatics increases the character'$ target number t0 be hit while making an Evade action adding his skiii bonus to the modifier for Evading successful Acrobatics Action Check will also help the character break his fall, reducing the damage taken by one wound level with successful Skill Check (see Falling; p. 1201.
APPRAISAL The   Appraisal Skill enables characters to  appraise the value of items_ Since some items are more easily appraised than others_ modifiers should be applied to Appraisal Checks based on how common Or rare the item IS; To determine the value of common items Or raw materials such as metals and gems apply 2 Modifier to the roll. For appraisals of uncom mon  materials Or items , apply 1 Modifier. Action Checks made t0 appraise very rare items_ such as Star League tech- nology or seventeenth-century furniture_ receive modifiers rang: ing from +1 to +3, depending o the rarity of the item_ If the Action Check fails_ it means that the character either identified the item incorrectly or misjudged its value_ The extent of the error depends on the Margin of Failure and the gamemas: ter'$ discretion rule of thumb is plus or minus percent per point by which the roll fails Knowing commodity'$ value does not mean the character will get full value tor when he tries t0 sell it or that he will be able t0 purchase it at its true worth: Negotiation Skill is used to determine the actual selling or purchase price
ACTING Among their repertoire of abilities, skilled actors can imitate particular people_ summon emotiona responses at will and con- vince their audience they are telling the truth when they are in fact lying: Obviously, this has numerous applications in game play: The difficulty modifier for an Acting Check is based on the condition of the target audience they are receptive t0 the show;" they will be an easier target than suspicious or hostile audience.
ADMINISTRATION This is the skill of running business, including the skills needed to manage large military unit; Characters with the Administration Skill may be allowed to operate business on the side with the gamemaster' approval: Such enterprises can provide the character with steady source of income as well as numerous roleplaying opportunities: The exact operation of the business should be worked out between the player and the  gamemaster: the simplest level, the gamemaster can have the character make quarterly  Action Check to determine the enterprise' earnings losses. Multiply the Margin of Success or Failure by OO0 to determine how many C-bills the business earned or lost  Alternatively; the player and gamemaster can devise as complicated system of finances as they wish. The level of detail is matter of taste.
ARCHERY This is the weapon skill used for making attacks with all kinds of bows, from short and long bows to crossbows Additionally; Archery Skill enables characters jury-rig badly damaged bow Or build simple bow from scratch. Jury-rig ging or building bow requires basic tools and takes about tnir: ty minutes_ Make an Archery Check at the end of this period: success means the bow is functional, though attacks made with suffer target number modifier equal to 8 minus the Margin of Success of the Check (to minimum of +1). Failure means the bow looks fine but will break the frst time is used_
ANIMAL HANDLING This skill covers all facets of animal care from feeding and grooming to breeding and training: Characters are most likely to want t0 use this Skill to train animals to help them on adven- tures While this can add a interesting element to the game practice animal training iS long and difficult process_ It is also hardly an exact science; years of training can still result in a uncooperative animal.
ARTILLERY This weapon skill is used to fire and spot for all kinds of artillery weapons_ from field artillery to artillery mounted vehicles or 'Mechs
